title: Alloy Formal Modeling Tool Chain
summary: A chain of formal modeling tools reimplemented in Python from scratch based on the research papers. Starting with Alloy's specification language and relational logic (Jackson, 2002), through Kodkod's SAT-based model finding engine with symmetry breaking (Torlak & Jackson, 2007), Pardinus's temporal logic extensions with decomposed solving (Macedo et al., 2016), to Electrum's full temporal specification analyzer with trace animation (Macedo et al., 2018). Each module builds incrementally on the previous, forming a complete formal verification pipeline.
instructionPreview: Implement a chain of formal modeling tools in Python FROM SCRATCH based on research papers. The workspace contains a base framework with relational data structures (Atom, TupleS, Universe, Relation, Bounds) and AST nodes for relational logic. Implement 4 modules in order:
instruction:
Implement a chain of formal modeling tools in Python FROM SCRATCH based on
research papers. The workspace contains a base framework with relational data
structures (Atom, TupleS, Universe, Relation, Bounds) and AST nodes for
relational logic. Implement 4 modules in order:

1. alloy/ - Alloy specification language: full AST model (Expression hierarchy
   with UnaryExpr/BinaryExpr/QuantExpr/CallExpr, Formula hierarchy with
   CompareFormula/QuantFormula/BoolFormula), Lark-based Earley parser for
   sig/field/fact/pred/fun/assert declarations with all relational operators
   (join/product/closure/transpose/override/restrict) and quantifiers, type
   checker with sig hierarchy analysis and field type inference, scope resolver
   computing atom allocation from multiplicities and scopes, constraint
   generator translating model structure into relational constraints.

2. kodkod/ - SAT-based relational model finder: SAT translator with
   relation-to-boolean-matrix encoding (variable allocation, unit clauses for
   lower bounds, Tseitin transformation for AND/OR gates), formula encoder
   translating subset/equality/cardinality/function/acyclicity constraints
   to CNF, symmetry detection via base partitioning (PART procedure) with
   LexLeader predicate generation, Minisat22 integration for solving and
   solution enumeration via blocking clauses.

3. pardinus/ - Temporal model finding: comprehensive temporal AST (10 LTL
   operators including past-time), bounded model checking by unrolling mutable
   relations across k time steps, decomposed solving (static partition solved
   first, temporal partition with fixed static), trace extraction with lasso
   detection and state change tracking, protocol verification examples.

4. electrum/ - Full temporal Alloy: extended parser with var keyword and
   priming (e'), trace model with lasso semantics (infinite trace
   representation), animation engine for step-by-step counterexample
   visualization with change highlighting, full analyzer supporting multiple
   modes (consistency/safety/liveness), temporal fact compiler classifying
   constraints into init/transition/invariant/liveness categories.

Each module has experiments producing KEY: VALUE output lines. Create run.sh
to execute all experiments. See requirements/ for detailed specifications.

Total implementation: ~4,770 lines of Python across 26 source files.

moduleDag.description: A chain of formal modeling tools reimplemented in Python from scratch based on the research papers. Starting with Alloy's specification language and relational logic (Jackson, 2002), through Kodkod's SAT-based model finding engine with symmetry breaking (Torlak & Jackson, 2007), Pardinus's temporal logic extensions with decomposed solving (Macedo et al., 2016), to Electrum's full temporal specification analyzer with trace animation (Macedo et al., 2018). Each module builds incrementally on the previous, forming a complete formal verification pipeline.

nodes.label: Alloy: A Lightweight Object Modelling Notation
nodes.description: Alloy specification language parser, type checker, scope resolver, and constraint generator. Parses signatures, fields, facts, assertions, and predicates with relational expressions and first-order quantifiers. Implements full expression hierarchy (UnaryExpr, BinaryExpr, QuantExpr, CallExpr), type inference over sig hierarchy, scope resolution with multiplicity constraints, and constraint generation for the solver.

nodes.label: Kodkod: A Relational Model Finder
nodes.description: SAT-based relational model finder. Translates relational logic formulas to boolean satisfiability via sparse matrix encoding. Includes Tseitin transformation for efficient CNF generation, symmetry detection via base partitioning algorithm with LexLeader predicate generation, formula encoding for subset/equality/cardinality/ function/acyclicity constraints, solution enumeration via blocking, and full integration with python-sat Minisat22.

nodes.label: Pardinus: A Temporal Relational Model Finder
nodes.description: Extends Kodkod with linear temporal logic (LTL + past operators). Implements bounded temporal model checking by unrolling traces and encoding temporal operators as static relational constraints. Features decomposed solving (static partition solved first, temporal with fixed static), trace extraction with lasso detection, and protocol verification (leader election, mutual exclusion).

nodes.label: Electrum: Temporal Alloy Analyzer
nodes.description: Full temporal specification analyzer combining Alloy's syntax with Pardinus's temporal model checking. Supports var keyword for mutable fields, temporal operators (always/eventually/after/before/ once/historically/until/since), primed expressions for next-state, trace model with lasso semantics, animation engine for step-by-step counterexample visualization, temporal fact compilation, and multiple analysis modes (consistency, safety, liveness, bounded).

edges.label: Kodkod serves as the efficient relational engine behind Alloy
Pardinus extends Kodkod with temporal and decomposed solving
Electrum uses Pardinus to add temporal model checking to Alloy
Electrum extends Alloy's parser with temporal constructs
